goal: Ship an isolated, reviewable card-profile-to-comparison workflow with an accessible picker and a readable mobile comparison table.
completed: Added a secondary Compare action to every card profile while preserving Confirm with issuer as the primary action.
Built a reusable native-dialog card picker with search, selected-card pinning, multi-selection, responsive three/four-card limits, Escape handling, focus return, and explicit discovery scope.
Made repeated cards query parameters the canonical comparison state, with allowlist validation, deduplication, stable ordering, a four-card cap, and shareable URLs.
Replaced the hard-coded three-card div matrix with an add/remove comparison flow and a semantic HTML table grouped by access, funding, cost, rewards, and qualification.
Replaced the mobile stacked-card layout with a localized horizontal table scroller, sticky factor column, visible 44px scroll controls, and no horizontal page overflow.
Updated the UI blueprint and persisted CardStats design system to record the approved mobile comparison behavior.
Deployed protected Vercel preview dpl_G5xxfvXCbRgBXYrFgQoEGbdDD47G for pull-request review.
nextActions: Review the isolated comparison-workflow pull request; merge it if accepted or close it without changing main if rejected.
After that review, resume independent review of the 24 official observations and normalize regional offering boundaries before promotion.
decisions: The profile picker is a bounded selection dialog, not primary navigation; navigation occurs only after the user confirms at least two cards.
Comparison state lives in repeated URL parameters so it is shareable, restorable, and server-rendered rather than hidden in transient client state.
Program slugs are validated against the published discovery catalog and never silently converted into placeholder or zero values.
Mobile preserves a real data table with readable columns inside a contained horizontal scroller; comparison data never becomes a grid of cards.
Regional offering and scenario matching remain explicitly unavailable until official review; the UI does not infer eligibility or rank mismatched observations.

passed: npm run check passes ESLint, strict TypeScript, 9 Vitest files with 23 tests including the database publication workflow, Prisma generation, and the 50-page Next.js production build.

Full Playwright suite passes 10 desktop/mobile tests covering picker selection, Escape and focus return, shareable URL navigation, semantic table structure, axe, reduced motion, localized scrolling, 375px, 812x375 landscape, and 200% text.
Desktop comparison, 375px mobile comparison, and desktop picker were visually inspected against the persisted CardStats design system.
The required ui-ux-pro-max validation search and canonical accessibility, touch, responsive, and interaction checklist were applied.
Protected preview cardstats-qvu8ae65m-aterli.vercel.app is READY; authenticated profile and shareable comparison smoke checks return HTTP 200.
notRun: Manual VoiceOver and NVDA testing is unavailable in this environment; native semantics, keyboard behavior, focus management, and axe passed.
No production deployment or alias promotion was performed; the isolated preview is review-only.

risks: The comparison still displays competitor-discovered observations, not verified regional offering terms; the scope boundary must remain visible until independent review.
The URL currently identifies card programs rather than normalized legal offering IDs; future regional modeling must migrate comparison identity without breaking shared links.
Native dialog behavior passed Chromium automation but still needs manual VoiceOver and NVDA confirmation before production release.
doNotRepeat: Do not present discovery observations as verified, rank on them, or silently normalize missing values to zero.
Do not replace the comparison table with mobile card grids or allow the table to create horizontal page overflow.
Do not add default comparison programs that could imply commercial preference.
Do not deploy or merge this branch unless the user approves the pull-request result.
